notes: S1 and S2 both score 8/8 for both versions — the skill refactor preserves all behavior
S3 failures are identical in nature (scope mismatch with real codebase) but different in which assertions fail
with_skill uses fewer tokens on S1/S2 (20k vs 22k) but significantly more on S3 (109k vs 61k) — the new skill version read reference files aggressively
with_skill was faster on average (-17s) despite higher token usage — reference file approach may reduce back-and-forth
The 78% line reduction (453 → 99) produced identical pass rates — shorter skill, same quality
